LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARL'S JR. RESTAURANTS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. ASSUMING ARGUENDO THAT DEFENDANT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION, TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT EXIST AS TO BOTH POINTS RAISED BY DEFENDANT'S MOTION. FIRST, AN EXPERT DECLARES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PROVIDE ADEQUATE WARNING. (PSS 7.) SECOND, A SECURITY GUARD DECLARES THAT SEVERAL PEOPLE HAD SLIPPED AND FALLEN ON WATER AT THE PREMISES AND HE INFORMED DEFENDANT'S MANAGEMENT. (PSS 10, 12.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Thomas W. Purtell, Esq. (415-722-6291) appearas as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, John C. Won of Norton & Melnik APC (925-718-1040) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CARL'S JR. RESTAURANTS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Assuming arguendo that defendant met its initial burden of production, triable issues of material fact exist as to both points raised by defendant's motion. First, an expert declares that defendant did not provide adequate warning. (PSS 7.) Second, a security guard declares that several people had slipped and fallen on water at the premises and he informed defendant's management. (PSS 10, 12.) Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
